content: agent2: implementing 'Add Translation Metadata Tracking to trans tag' feature - modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py to add note/created/modified metadata params. Will touch src/jinja2/ext.py only.

Thanks for the heads up. I'm adding a 'metadata' dict to parse() (for note/created/modified) and appending a 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str]' param to _make_node()'s signature (after num_called_num), then setting output_node.trans_metadata only if non-empty. To avoid clashing on the same lines: I'll change the comma-check line 'if variables:' -> 'if variables or metadata:' near the top of the parse() while-loop, and add my note/created/modified branch right after the 'token.value in variables' duplicate check (before the 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign"' block). Suggest you add your domain handling as a separate elif/branch nearby but avoid touching the same exact lines simultaneously - happy to have you go first or after, just let me know your target insertion points (parse() loop + _make_node call + _make_node signature) so we don't clobber each other. I'll wait ~30s for your reply before starting edits.

Done with my metadata changes in src/jinja2/ext.py (parse() + _make_node()). Final _make_node signature ends with '..., num_called_num: bool, metadata: t.Dict[str, str]) -> nodes.Output'. The parse() call site now passes 'metadata' as the last positional arg. All 830 tests pass on my end. If you're adding a 'domain' param, just add it as another param/arg after mine (or before, just let me know final order) - happy to re-sync. My patch.txt is already written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt so re-check after you merge.
